footer.tagline: Web Development · Dog Training · Game Development

main.lead1: I design websites, study the relationship between people and dogs, and experiment with game development.
main.lead2: Three different paths, one direction: observe, simplify and build something that truly works.

manifesto.title: Three directions. One way of working.
manifesto.intro: I'm not interested in adding complexity where it isn't needed. In every project I start with the essentials: understanding the context, observing what matters, and building an experience that's simpler to live with.

clarity.context: On the web I look for
clarity.desc: Understand the context, remove the superfluous and make every page easy to read.

comprehension.desc: Observe the individual, listen to their signals and build trust, one step at a time.

interaction.context: In video games I look for
interaction.desc: Turn an idea into a mechanic, and a mechanic into an experience worth playing.

web.title: Websites that tell who you really are.
web.intro: For over 8 years I've been designing and developing showcase websites for professionals, local businesses and companies. I create digital experiences that are tidy, fast and built to help visitors immediately grasp one thing: why choose you.
web.mockupAltDesktop: Desktop mockup of a site developed by LeMaks
web.mockupAltMobile: Mobile mockup of a site developed by LeMaks
web.block1Title: I design sites that work before they look good.
web.block1Muted: An effective site doesn't just need good design. It must guide, explain, reassure and lead people toward a concrete action.

showcase.title: Showcase sites
showcase.desc: For professionals and businesses who want to tell their story clearly.

corporate.title: Corporate sites
corporate.desc: For companies that need a credible and well-organised online presence.

landing.title: Landing pages
landing.desc: For services, campaigns, products or ideas to be presented directly.

restyling.title: Restyling
restyling.desc: To turn a dated site into a clearer, faster and more up-to-date tool.

web.block2Title: Strategy, design and development in the same path.
web.block2Muted: Every project starts with a simple question: what should a person understand when they land on your site? From there I build structure, content, design and development.

process.05: Publish a site ready to grow

game.title: I build games for the web. Now I'm also exploring Godot.
game.intro1: I've already created games as web apps, turning ideas and mechanics into experiences accessible right from the browser.
game.intro2: Today I'm digging deeper into Godot to approach game development in a more structured way: prototyping, game design, physics, interactions and building small playable worlds.

browserBlock.title: From the browser to the game engine.
browserBlock.p1: My journey in game development started on the web: games and small interactive experiences built to be immediate, accessible and playable anywhere.
browserBlock.p2: Now I'm studying Godot to broaden the way I design games, experimenting with tools designed specifically for game development.
browserBlock.p3: I'm not starting from scratch: I bring the experience I built in web development, interface design and creating clear interactions.

webApp.title: Web app games
webApp.desc: Interactive experiences accessible from the browser, designed to be easy to launch and engaging to play.

prototypes.title: Prototypes and mechanics
prototypes.desc: Small experiments to test ideas, rules, pacing and game feedback.

godot.title: Godot projects
godot.desc: New exercises and prototypes built to deepen my 2D development, GDScript, physics and level design.

spotlight.title: A word game, designed for the screen in your hand.
spotlight.p1: A small web game inspired by guess-the-word games: simple to understand, quick to play and designed to work best on a smartphone.
spotlight.p2: The goal is to guess the right word through attempts, hints and visual feedback. Every choice must be immediate, readable and satisfying.
spotlight.note: The game is mainly optimised for mobile devices. The screens shown reflect the smartphone experience.

projects.title: Ideas that become playable.
projects.muted1: Here I gather web games, prototypes and projects in development.
projects.muted2: Some are born to explore a mechanic, others to create a complete experience. Each project is a chance to better understand how interaction, pacing, difficulty and fun really work.

proto.imageAlt: Visual concept of the first web game
proto.title: First Godot game
proto.desc: An interactive experience built for the browser, between simple mechanics and game pacing.
proto.aiNote: Visual concept generated with AI, used as a placeholder to convey the idea and atmosphere of the game. Not final artwork.

vision.title: Building experiences that invite you to play.
vision.p1: I want to keep building games for the web and, at the same time, grow my skills with Godot.
vision.p2: The goal is to combine what I already know about the web with tools and logic specific to game development, to create more curated, interactive and memorable experiences.

cinofila.title: Understanding the dog is the first step in training them.
cinofila.intro: I'm studying the cognitive-relational method to learn to observe the dog as an individual: with emotions, needs, experiences and their own way of communicating. A learning journey carried out with seriousness, study and respect.
cinofila.photoAlt3: A moment of connection with the dog

approach.title: Every dog has a story. That's where we begin.
approach.p1Before: There are no standard solutions that work for everyone. Every path starts from
approach.p1After: of the dog, the context they live in and the needs of their family.
approach.p2After: . The goal isn't a perfect dog, but a dog who is calmer, listened to and able to face the world alongside their person.

topics.title: Small steps, useful in real life.

puppies.title: Puppies and first months
puppies.desc: Routines, settling into the family and building the first foundations.
puppies.hint: Building calm habits right from the start.

communication.title: Communication
communication.desc: Learning to read signals, needs and moments of difficulty.

walking.title: Walking and environment
walking.desc: Creating calmer experiences inside and outside the home.
walking.hint: The environment is part of the education.

adopted.title: Adopted dogs
adopted.desc: Welcoming the dog's history and gradually building new habits.

diary.title: I study, observe, learn.
diary.desc: This space gathers notes, reflections and observations from my learning journey. It doesn't aim to give absolute answers: it wants to share better questions and a more mindful way of being alongside dogs.

articles.title: I write about what I'm building.
articles.p1: Here I collect articles, technical notes and deep dives born from my projects.
articles.p2: It's not auto-published content: I only pick the ones that best represent how I work, the technologies I use and the ideas I want to share.

featured.title: Featured article
featured.articleTitle: Enhancing React Development with Dynamic Styled Components and a Custom Grid System
featured.articleDesc: A deep dive into how to use custom functions and utilities with styled-components to manage colours, spacing, breakpoints and a tailor-made responsive grid system.

about.title: I design, observe, experiment.
about.p1: I'm Maksim, a web developer with over 8 years of experience. The web is my main job: I design showcase sites to help businesses, professionals and companies tell their story better online.
about.p2: Alongside work, I'm exploring two paths that are teaching me a lot: cognitive-relational dog training and game development with Godot.
about.p3: They're different fields, but they have something in common: they require listening, observation, patience and the ability to build experiences that make sense for those who live them.

services.title: What I can do for you
services.subtitle: I work with local businesses, associations, events and small operations. Drop me a line to see if I can help.

local.title: Sites for events and local businesses
local.desc: Fast, mobile and easy-to-share landing pages: schedule, maps, contacts, sign-ups and social links. All in one place, so information doesn't stay scattered between posts and flyers.

automation.title: Automations to reduce manual work
automation.desc: Forms, requests, confirmations, reminders, management sheets and small processes wired together. Fewer messages to handle by hand, more time for the work that matters.

services.cta: Let's talk about your site or your automation →

contact.title: Let's talk about your site or your automation
contact.subtitle: Do you have an event, a business or a process you want to make easier to run? Drop me a line: I reply within 24 hours to figure out together if I can help.
contact.privacyNote: The data entered will be used exclusively to reply to your contact request and will not be used for marketing purposes without your consent.
